spec_match.rationale: The demo uses standard getUserMedia under the hood and falsely detects native <usermedia> support using customElements.get which is invalid for native tags.

interactive.rationale: Allows configuring attributes with live-updating generated markup, constraints, and a test button that starts the webcam.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Correctly explains and explores video/audio/facingMode attributes on the declarative element.

edge_cases.rationale: Displays output error messages if media access is denied, but doesn't handle cases where no camera/mic is present gracefully.

references.rationale: Cites the ChromeStatus feature entry correctly.

design_system.rationale: Follows the design system well with nice attribute panels, generated code snippets, and clean preview area.

openQuestions.title: Fix native element support detection
openQuestions.detail: The demo uses `customElements.get('usermedia')` to check if the tag is supported, but native elements are not registered in the customElements registry. This should check `window.HTMLUserMediaElement` or test the element's constructor name.

openQuestions.title: Integrate true <usermedia> activation
openQuestions.detail: Instead of using standard getUserMedia as a simulator, the demo should dynamically construct a real <usermedia> element based on current attributes and let the user click the actual element to test it when native support is present.

summary: A functional explorer that shows how <usermedia> attributes map to getUserMedia constraints, but uses incorrect native support detection and doesn't actually instantiate a real element.
